name: Service Desk Manager
description: Captures demand for incident resolution and service requests, serves as single point of contact for the service provider with all users.

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions service desk, catalog, tickets, service requests, or help desk

actions.name: manage-ticket
actions.description: Create, update, query, route, or close tickets across all channels

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: create, update, query, route, close

actions.name: search-knowledge-base
actions.description: Search knowledge articles and FAQ for first-contact resolution

actions.name: kedb-lookup
actions.description: Search known errors and approved workarounds

actions.name: manage-self-service-portal
actions.description: Manage portal content, virtual agent configuration, and catalog items

actions.name: lookup-user
actions.description: Look up user profiles, entitlements, VIP status, and contact history

outputs.name: userProfile
outputs.description: User profile with entitlements and history

actions.name: send-user-notification
actions.description: Send user communications, status updates, and survey requests

actions.name: browse-service-catalog
actions.description: Browse and initiate standard service request fulfillment workflows

actions.name: view-desk-dashboard
actions.description: Track service desk KPIs: FCR rate, CSAT, abandonment, handle time

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: Service desk logs incidents and routes to incident management; receives status for user updates
Service desk communicates planned changes to users; receives change-related contacts
Knowledge articles enable first-contact resolution; service desk identifies knowledge gaps
SLA targets drive service desk response and escalation timers; desk feeds SLA metrics
CMDB provides user-CI mapping for ticket context and impact identification
Service desk initiates and tracks service request fulfillment
Proactive alerts enable service desk to communicate before users report issues

hitlControls.reason: Contacts from VIP users require human review of response quality
Mass communication to users about service disruptions requires human approval
Management escalation requires human judgment and approval
Negative satisfaction scores triggering service recovery require human engagement
New portal articles or virtual agent flows require human review before activation
Modifications to ticket routing rules require human approval

knowledgeSources.description: Tickets, routing rules, and queue data
Knowledge articles and FAQs for first-contact resolution
User profiles, entitlements, and contact history
